scope_note: QA Cassini Alpha Parity Gate cert [418]. Primary sources: Cassini, J.-D. (1680) 'Une nouvelle progression de nombres' Histoire de l'Academie Royale des Sciences (original Cassini identity F_{n-1}*F_{n+1} - F_n^2 = (-1)^n, the foundational sign-alternation identity for the Fibonacci sequence); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition and Pisano period for prime moduli); Lehmer, D.H. (1930) 'An extended theory of Lucas functions' doi:10.2307/1968235 (divisibility theory for Lucas sequences including period and rank). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The parity of alpha(p) (the QA T-step first-zero time, cert [416]) is constrained by the quadratic character of -1 mod p, via the Cassini identity evaluated at n=alpha(p). (C1) CASSINI GATE IDENTITY: F_{alpha(p)-1}^2 ≡ (-1)^{alpha(p)} mod p for all 45 tested primes; proved algebraically: Cassini at n=alpha(p) gives F_{alpha-1}*F_{alpha+1} - F_{alpha}^2 = (-1)^{alpha}; since F_{alpha}≡0 mod p, F_{alpha+1}=F_{alpha}+F_{alpha-1}≡F_{alpha-1} mod p; substituting: F_{alpha-1}^2 ≡ (-1)^{alpha} mod p; verified computationally for all 45 primes (all correct); (C2) PARITY CONSTRAINT p≡3 mod 4 IMPLIES alpha EVEN: for all 24 primes p≡3 mod 4 in test set, alpha(p) is even; proof: if alpha(p) odd then C1 gives F_{alpha-1}^2 ≡ -1 mod p, so -1 is a quadratic residue mod p, so p≡1 mod 4 by Euler's criterion; contrapositive: p≡3 mod 4 → alpha(p) even; no counterexample found; falsifiable by any prime p≡3 mod 4 with odd rank of apparition; (C3) ODD ALPHA GIVES EXPLICIT SQRT(-1): for all 13 primes with odd alpha(p) (all satisfying p≡1 mod 4): F_{alpha(p)-1} is an explicit square root of -1 mod p, computed by QA T-step iteration (pure integer, Theorem NT); for p=193 alpha=97: F_96^2≡192=-1 mod 193, i.e. F_96 is a sqrt(-1) mod 193; for p=13 alpha=7: F_6=8, 8^2=64≡12=-1 mod 13; for p=37 alpha=19: F_18^2≡-1 mod 37; the QA T-step implicitly computes sqrt(-1) for all such primes without any square-root algorithm; (C4) ORBIT PARITY READOUT: the second component of the QA T-orbit at step alpha(p) (= F_{alpha(p)+1} ≡ F_{alpha(p)-1} mod p) squared gives (-1)^{alpha(p)} mod p; this means the parity of alpha(p) is readable from the orbit state at step alpha without knowing alpha in advance; verified for 5 sample primes (193 inert odd-alpha, 37 inert odd-alpha, 41 split even-alpha, 11 split even-alpha, 7 inert even-alpha). NOTE ON delta(p)=1 for p=193: in cert [417], the Wall-Sun-Sun Fermat quotient delta(193)=1 means F_{97}≡193 mod 193^2; in ℤ[phi]/193^2, phi^{97}≡F_96 + 193*phi where F_96^2≡-1 mod 193 (C3); so phi^{alpha(193)} has the form i + p*phi where i is a sqrt(-1) mod p; delta(p)=1 means the p-coefficient of the p^2-expansion of phi^{alpha} is exactly 1 (minimum non-zero); other primes with delta=1 include Fibonacci primes (p=F_k, where F_{alpha}=p exactly) and p=41 (where 41|L_10, even alpha, different mechanism); the deeper characterization of delta=1 primes remains open. CHAIN: [416] alpha(p)|p-(5/p), rank of apparition -> [417] alpha(p^2)=p*alpha(p), Wall-Sun-Sun lift -> [418] alpha(p) parity ↔ (-1) quadratic residuosity (Cassini Gate). All integer arithmetic. C2 is independently falsifiable (one prime p≡3 mod 4 with odd alpha refutes). Derived 2026-06-14.
